$comment: CANONICAL SOURCE for the Xylem habit layer. Do not edit the rendered outputs directly -- artifacts/claude_md_block.md, artifacts/xylem_discipline.md, and plugin/artifacts/discipline.md are all generated from this file by scripts/render_discipline.py, and tests/test_generated_sync.py fails if they drift. Run: python scripts/render_discipline.py --write
loop: Survey board -> claim touches -> load memory (recall) -> work (raise judgment calls) -> record decisions -> release with closing note -> distill captures
preamble_block: This machine runs the Xylem suite (context-keeper, agentsync, cambium). Follow this discipline:
preamble_command: Follow this workflow for the whole session. The loop:
preamble_plugin: This session is running with the Xylem stack: a memory that persists, coordination across agents, and a loop that compounds what you learn. Work the habit, not just the task.

rules.title: Survey the board
rules.body: survey the agentsync board. Read who is active and what is already claimed. Do not touch a file another agent holds without asking first.

rules.title: Claim your touches
rules.body: claim every file or area the pre-approved task will modify. Never force over an active peer's claim without asking first. On claims running longer than ~15 minutes, post brief `update_status` notes at milestones -- 2 to 4 per claim, no more.

rules.title: Load memory
rules.body: a context-keeper project summary is injected via hook. If one was not injected, pull `get_project_summary` before starting work. Treat any active constraints it lists as binding. Do not re-litigate settled decisions -- if one looks wrong, raise it (via the agentsync-remote `mailbox` tool if that Worker is configured, otherwise ask the user); don't silently override it.

rules.title: Recall what the team already learned
rules.body: `recall()` relevant knowledge from cambium for the task at hand -- past outcomes, gotchas, and decisions distilled from prior sessions -- so you build on what the team already learned instead of rediscovering it.

rules.title: Work, and raise judgment calls
rules.lead: On judgment calls with multiple defensible answers
rules.body: raise the question (via the agentsync-remote `mailbox` tool if that Worker is configured, otherwise ask the user) and continue on non-dependent work rather than blocking. Do the pre-approved work and nothing outside it.

rules.title: Record decisions as you make them
rules.lead: When you make an architectural or design decision
rules.body: record it in context-keeper with its rationale at the moment it's made -- the problem, why this option won, the alternatives, the tradeoffs -- not in a batch at the end.

rules.title: Release with a closing note
rules.body: record any new decisions, then release each claim with a closing note stating the outcome and the recommended next action. For a build session, the definition of done includes PUSHED TO ORIGIN, not just committed -- verify the push in the closing note.

rules.title: Capture (mostly automatic)
rules.body: capture is passive: cambium's `distill()` runs from the SessionEnd hook, turning done agentsync claims and context-keeper decisions into memory. If that hook is not installed, run `distill()` yourself as work lands, and `promote()` knowledge that has earned recalls so the team and org see it.

block_footer: Run `/xylem-discipline` to load the full workflow for a session.

command_frontmatter.description: Load the Xylem coordination + memory workflow for this session

command_args_note:
**Pre-approved work for this session:** $ARGUMENTS

If the slot above is empty, ask the user what the pre-approved task is before claiming anything.
plugin_footer: Recall, claim, record, release. Small, honest, compounding. The point is that the next session - yours or a teammate's - starts smarter than this one did.
